---
title: "FedRAMP Boundaries — Articles on Application-Aware Networking"
subtitle: "A collected series by Michal Doroszewski"
author: "Michal Doroszewski"
date: "2026-04-17"
categories: [federal-modernization, fedramp-boundaries, application-aware-networking, book]
image: "images/intro.png"
---

{{< include ../../_aspirational-notice.qmd >}}

![](images/intro.png)

This collection brings together twenty-one articles on **Application-Aware Networking** — a growing and largely unspoken problem in federal cloud architecture: the absence of meaningful visibility, telemetry, and adaptive control in the networks that agencies depend on every day.

The series is structured in two parts.

- **Part I — The Problems** (Articles 1–10) identifies the core failures, from the blindfold that hides what is happening inside the network, to the failures of trust, identity, policy, and optimization that compound when infrastructure lacks the ability to observe and respond to application behavior.
- **Part II — The Layers** (Articles 11–21) introduces a layered architecture for solving these problems. Each layer builds on the one before it, starting with visibility and continuity, progressing through control, signaling, evaluation, and decision-making, and culminating in automation, remediation, recovery, stability, and resilience.

Together these articles argue that **the network must become aware of the applications it carries**, and that building this awareness requires a deliberate, structured, and layered approach.

## Prologue

- [What We Don't Even Know We're Missing](what-we-dont-know-were-missing.qmd)

## Part I — The Problems

1. [1. The Blindfold Problem](article-01-blindfold-problem.qmd)
2. [2. The Boundary Problem](article-02-boundary-problem.qmd)
3. [3. The Distance Problem](article-03-distance-problem.qmd)
4. [4. The Identity Problem](article-04-identity-problem.qmd)
5. [5. The Arbitrariness Problem](article-05-arbitrariness-problem.qmd)
6. [6. The Telemetry Problem](article-06-telemetry-problem.qmd)
7. [7. The Optimization Problem](article-07-optimization-problem.qmd)
8. [8. The Media Problem](article-08-media-problem.qmd)
9. [9. The Trust Problem](article-09-trust-problem.qmd)
10. [10. The Policy Problem](article-10-policy-problem.qmd)

## Part II — The Layers

11. [11. The Visibility Layer](article-11-visibility-layer.qmd)
12. [12. The Continuity Layer](article-12-continuity-layer.qmd)
13. [13. The Control Layer](article-13-control-layer.qmd)
14. [14. The Signal Layer](article-14-signal-layer.qmd)
15. [15. The Evaluation Layer](article-15-evaluation-layer.qmd)
16. [16. The Decision Layer](article-16-decision-layer.qmd)
17. [17. The Automation Layer](article-17-automation-layer.qmd)
18. [18. The Remediation Layer](article-18-remediation-layer.qmd)
19. [19. The Recovery Layer](article-19-recovery-layer.qmd)
20. [20. The Stability Layer](article-20-stability-layer.qmd)
21. [21. The Resilience Layer](article-21-resilience-layer.qmd)

## UIAO canon alignment

This book is **Series 1 + Series 5** of the UIAO modernization canon:

- **Series 1 — Federal Modernization Architecture** reads Part I as its foundational diagnostic arc.
- **Series 5 — Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay Architecture** reads Part II as its architectural body.

Per [ADR-028](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/core/canon/adr/adr-028-monorepo-consolidation-gos-integration.md) and the pre-UIAO promotion path (ADR-030), these articles were drafted before the UIAO substrate was formalized; they are published here with byline and body preserved, filename qualifiers dropped, and one title disambiguated (Article 05 renamed from "The Boundary Problem" to "The Arbitrariness Problem" to resolve a collision with Article 02).

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Provenance
Source: `inbox/Book of Articles - Application Aware Networking.docx` and the 21 individual article files from the round-2 drop on 2026-04-17.
:::
